title: Daily Market Digest: Geopolitical Tensions & Oil Surge Drive Sell-Off
subtitle: Market insights for Wednesday, July 8, 2026

items.headline: Global Markets Decline Amid Renewed US-Iran Tensions and Surging Oil Prices
items.summary: U.S. equity markets experienced a broad sell-off today as President Donald Trump declared the ceasefire with Iran 'over,' escalating geopolitical tensions. This development sent crude oil prices sharply higher, fueling concerns over inflation and prompting investors to pull out of riskier assets. Technology and travel sectors were particularly hard-hit, while energy and consumer defensive stocks showed resilience. The International Monetary Fund (IMF) also downgraded its global economic growth outlook for 2026, citing the energy shock from the conflict. All eyes were on the Federal Reserve's release of the June FOMC meeting minutes, with investors keenly analyzing clues regarding future interest rate trajectories amidst persistent inflation.

items.headline: Crinetics Pharmaceuticals, Inc. Surges Over 115%
items.summary: Crinetics Pharmaceuticals Inc. ($CRNX) was among the top performers, demonstrating significant strength with a gain of 115.95%.

items.headline: Broadcom Rises on Apple Partnership
items.summary: Broadcom ($AVGO) saw its shares climb by 4.5% in early trading, following Apple's announcement of a multiyear commitment to design and produce custom components with the company, an agreement valued at over $30 billion.

items.headline: Cognizant Technology Solutions Rallies on Google Cloud Partnership
items.summary: Cognizant Technology Solutions Corp. ($CTSH) rallied 6.2% after expanding its partnership with Google Cloud for enterprise adoption of Gemini AI.

items.headline: Elicio Therapeutics Plunges on Direct Offering
items.summary: Elicio Therapeutics, Inc. ($ELTX) experienced a sharp decline of 37.35% following the announcement of a $15 million registered direct offering.

items.headline: Rivian Automotive Shares Dive on Stock Sale
items.summary: Rivian Automotive Inc.'s ($RIVN) shares plunged 18.1% after the company decided to sell 75 million shares of its Class A common stock.

items.headline: Intel Drops as Tech Sector Falters
items.summary: Intel Corporation ($INTC) saw its shares drop over 5% as the broader technology sector faltered amidst market anxieties.

items.headline: Fed Minutes Released: Focus on Inflation and Geopolitical Impact
items.summary: The Federal Reserve released the minutes from its June 17 FOMC meeting today, revealing insights into the committee's decision to hold the federal funds rate steady at 3.50%–3.75%. Investors are scrutinizing the minutes for clues on how policymakers are balancing elevated inflation—running at roughly twice the 2% target—with the economic effects of the Middle East conflict.

items.headline: IMF Downgrades Global Growth Outlook Amid Energy Shock
items.summary: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) modestly downgraded its outlook for the global economy in 2026, projecting a sluggish 3% expansion, down from previous forecasts. The revision is primarily attributed to the energy shock resulting from the ongoing conflict in Iran, though booming investment in artificial intelligence is partially offsetting the fallout. The IMF expects oil prices to climb nearly 32% this year and global consumer prices to increase 4.7%, indicating a stall in inflation progress.

items.headline: Energy Sector Leads Gains as Oil Prices Soar
items.summary: With renewed US-Iran tensions driving West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude oil up 7.85% to almost $76 a barrel and Brent crude climbing 4.8% to $77.74, the energy sector emerged as a leading performer. Companies like Exxon Mobil ($XOM), Chevron ($CVX), ConocoPhillips ($COP), and Valero Energy ($VLO) saw gains.

items.headline: Technology and Travel Sectors Face Significant Headwinds
items.summary: The technology sector, particularly chipmakers, and travel-related companies experienced notable declines. Tech stocks were pressured by the broader market downturn and negative reports from international chipmakers. Airline and cruise line stocks fell due to concerns over higher fuel costs.

items.headline: CHS Inc. Reports Strong Q3 Earnings Driven by Energy Segment
items.summary: CHS Inc. ($CHS) reported net income of $267.4 million and revenues of $11.6 billion for its third fiscal quarter of 2026. The company's energy segment benefited from robust refining margins, largely offsetting record-high renewable energy credit costs.

items.headline: Levi Strauss & Co. (LEVI) Among Companies Reporting Today
items.summary: Levi Strauss & Co. ($LEVI) is among the companies releasing their Q2 2026 earnings reports today. Investors will be closely watching sales performance across key regions and direct-to-consumer trends for insights into consumer demand for apparel.
